title: 900 Conclusions
definition: Pico's massive thesis system and public debate machine, requiring cluster-level source and doctrine mapping.

title: Abulafia
definition: The ecstatic Kabbalist whose materials enter Pico through translation and Christian transformation.

title: Act and Potency
definition: Aristotelian vocabulary crucial for reading Pico's anthropology of indeterminate potentiality and metaphysical ascent.

title: Active Intellect
definition: The Arabic and Aristotelian concept central to debates over conjunction, felicity, and human perfection.

title: Al-Farabi
definition: Arabic philosopher relevant to political, intellectual, and felicity traditions behind Pico's source ecology.

title: Anagogy
definition: The upward interpretive movement by which symbols, Scripture, and hierarchy lead the intellect toward God.

title: Angelology
definition: The systematic study of angelic hierarchy, intellect, imitation, separate substances, and Kabbalistic angelic orders in Pico.

title: Angelology Taxonomy
definition: PicoDB now separates angelic imitation, hierarchy, angelic mind, intelligible world, Kabbalistic angels, Arabic intellect, Thomistic separate substances, and grace.

title: Angels
definition: Created intellectual beings and models of human ascent that connect Dionysius, Aquinas, Proclus, Kabbalah, and Arabic intellect theory.

title: Apology
definition: Pico's defense of condemned theses and the key juridical-theological witness for his dangerous formulations.

title: Apophasis
definition: Negative theology and the disciplined recognition that divine reality exceeds names, concepts, and ordinary predication.

title: Aquinas
definition: The scholastic authority through whom Pico disciplines being, unity, creation, names, angels, and Christian metaphysics.

title: Aristotle
definition: The philosopher whose concord with Plato Pico repeatedly asserts, tests, and reconfigures across metaphysics and natural philosophy.

title: Ascent
definition: The movement from lower life through intellectual and angelic forms toward God, joining ethics, metaphysics, Kabbalah, and prayer.

title: Astral Magic
definition: The field where Ficinian celestial mediation, images, music, spiritus, and Pico's later astrology critique must be carefully separated.

title: Astrology
definition: The science, art, and divinatory practice Pico uses, limits, and attacks across his career.

title: Astrology Taxonomy
definition: PicoDB now separates natural celestial influence, judicial astrology, astral magic, poetic astrology, Kabbalistic correspondences, biblical cosmology, anti-astrology, and reception.

title: Averroes
definition: Arabic commentator on Aristotle whose theories of intellect and felicity enter Pico's thesis and Heptaplus fields.

title: Avicenna
definition: Arabic philosopher whose metaphysics, psychology, and intellect theory help shape Pico's source network.

title: Beauty
definition: The intelligible and sensible radiance of form that connects love theory, metaphysics, and poetic theology.

title: Being
definition: The metaphysical term at the center of Pico's effort to reconcile Plato, Aristotle, Aquinas, and Dionysian transcendence.
pico_loci: On Being and Unity, 900 Conclusions, Oration

title: Benivieni
definition: Poet, collaborator, translator, and mediator whose canzone occasions the Commento and whose later activity shapes Pico reception.

title: Bereshit
definition: The Hebrew opening of Genesis and a concentrated locus for Pico's Kabbalistic and Mosaic hermeneutics.

title: Celestial Influence
definition: The disputed natural action of stars and heavens on lower bodies, love, medicine, and events.

title: Chaldean Oracles
definition: Ancient theological verses used in Renaissance Platonism and Pico's esoteric source environment.

title: Christian Kabbalah in Pico
definition: Pico's Kabbalah depends on translation, Hebrew aspiration, Christian interpretation, and the ambitious integration of Jewish mystical materials into concordist philosophy.

title: Christology
definition: The doctrine of Christ as it shapes Pico's anthropology, Kabbalah, Heptaplus, and theological defenses.

title: Commento
definition: Pico's commentary on Benivieni's canzone, a love-metaphysics and anti-Ficinian text with a complex transmission history.

title: Commento Reading Ontology
definition: A new ontology layer tells future summaries what to mark in every Commento chapter and stanza.

title: Commento Translation Collation
definition: A workflow separates controlling primary text, early modern reception, and restricted modern translation witness.

title: Concord
definition: Pico's practice of testing agreement among Plato, Aristotle, scholasticism, Kabbalah, magic, and theology without erasing difference.
pico_loci: 900 Conclusions, On Being and Unity, Oration

title: Condemned Theses
definition: The thirteen theses judged suspect or dangerous in the Roman crisis and defended in the Apology.

title: Corpus Control Dashboard
definition: A new dashboard distinguishes OCR control witnesses, reception witnesses, public digital leads, manuscript gaps, and related non-Giovanni texts.
pico_loci: What is acquired, controlled, or still missing

title: Correspondence
definition: The relation linking levels of reality, symbols, names, stars, angels, and human ascent.

title: Creation
definition: The metaphysical and biblical problem of how all things proceed from God and are intelligible through hierarchy and Scripture.

title: De amore
definition: Ficino's commentary/treatise on Plato's Symposium and the main comparator for Pico's Commento.

title: Dignity
definition: The famous but often mythologized concept in the Oration that must be read inside ascent, discipline, hierarchy, and the 900 Conclusions.

title: Dionysius
definition: Pseudo-Dionysius as authority for hierarchy, apophasis, symbol, anagogy, and Christian Neoplatonic ascent.
pico_loci: Oration, On Being and Unity, Heptaplus

title: Disputationes
definition: Pico's late attack on divinatory astrology and a major textual-transmission problem in the posthumous corpus.

title: Divine Names
definition: Names of God as theological, Kabbalistic, magical, and philological objects in Pico's claims.

title: Eurydice
definition: The mythic figure through whom Pico's Commento sharpens death-to-imagination and ascent beyond lower cognition.

title: Eyn-Sof
definition: The infinite divine principle in Kabbalah, requiring strict source control before use in Pico interpretation.

title: Felicity
definition: The goal of intellectual and spiritual life, debated through Aristotelian, Arabic, Christian, and biblical frameworks.

title: Ficinian Love Platonism
definition: Jayne helps PicoDB distinguish Ficino as translator, Ficino as source of love Platonism, and Pico as a mediator/corrector.

title: Ficino
definition: Marsilio Ficino as translator, mentor, co-Platonist, love theorist, and target of Pico's correction.
pico_loci: Commento, On Being and Unity, Heptaplus

title: Forty-Nine Gates
definition: The Kabbalistic structure of understanding that shapes Pico's final Heptaplus architecture.

title: Four Worlds
definition: Pico's ordering of angelic, celestial, elemental, and human worlds, especially in Heptaplus interpretation.

title: Free Will
definition: Pico's account of human self-formation, not as modern autonomy but as metaphysical potentiality within a created hierarchy.

title: Gematria
definition: Letter-number interpretation that supports some Kabbalistic and symbolic arguments in Pico's source field.

title: Genesis
definition: The biblical text Pico reads as cosmology, anthropology, angelology, and hidden philosophy in the Heptaplus.

title: Gersonides
definition: Jewish philosopher and exegete relevant to Genesis, intellect, and biblical hermeneutics in Pico's Heptaplus context.

title: Gianfrancesco Pico
definition: Pico's nephew, editor, biographer, and possible mediator of posthumous textual and religious framing.

title: Hebrew
definition: The source language and symbolic medium through which Pico's Kabbalah, divine names, and philology must be controlled.

title: Heptaplus
definition: Pico's sevenfold Genesis commentary and the central text for biblical hermeneutics, cosmic order, and post-crisis recoding.

title: Heresy
definition: The juridical-theological danger field around the 900 Conclusions, Apology, magic, Kabbalah, and doctrinal language.

title: Hermes Trismegistus
definition: The ancient-theology figure whose Hermetic authority helps Renaissance thinkers imagine a prisca sapientia before Greek philosophy.

title: Hierarchy
definition: Ordered levels of being, knowledge, angels, worlds, and symbols across Pico's metaphysics and exegesis.

title: Iamblichus
definition: Neoplatonist associated with theurgy, hierarchy, and ancient wisdom in the 900 Conclusions source field.

title: Ibn Bajja
definition: Arabic philosopher tied to intellect, solitude, and felicity debates relevant to Pico's Heptaplus source field.

title: Idea of Man
definition: Allen's key term for Pico's anthropology, where the human being's indeterminacy and perfection become Christological in the Heptaplus.

title: Ideas
definition: The divine or intelligible exemplars through which Pico thinks form, beauty, creation, and the Idea of Man.

title: Imagination
definition: A faculty involved in love, ascent, error, images, magic, and the boundary between intellect and body.

title: Indeterminacy
definition: The condition of Adam as unfixed image and metaphysical potential, central to non-modern readings of the Oration.

title: Intellect
definition: The cognitive and ontological principle linking human knowing, angelic mind, Arabic philosophy, and felicity.

title: Jubilee
definition: A symbolic and Kabbalistic structure in the Heptaplus tied to sevenfold order, completion, and liberation.

title: Kabbalah
definition: The Jewish mystical and exegetical source field that Pico translates, Christianizes, and uses for theology, magic, and concord.

title: Love
definition: A central philosophical, cosmological, and theological force in Ficino and Pico, requiring distinction among personal, cosmic, divine, and intellectual registers.

title: Macrocosm
definition: The great world whose structure is mirrored, interpreted, and perfected through human and Christological order.

title: Magic
definition: The natural, angelic, Kabbalistic, and theological practice-field that made some of Pico's theses dangerous.

title: Maimonides
definition: Jewish philosopher whose biblical interpretation and negative theology form part of Pico's Jewish philosophical environment.

title: Melancholy
definition: A Renaissance medical and philosophical condition linked to genius, Saturn, imagination, and Ficinian context.

title: Microcosm
definition: The human being as little world and interpretive key to cosmic order.

title: Mind
definition: The intelligible level of reality, often called mens or intellectus, where Ideas, angelic mind, and metaphysical mediation become contested.

title: Missing Writings Taxonomy
definition: PicoDB now separates full primary text, partial primary text, secondary-rich gaps, review-only evidence, early-print needs, manuscript needs, and reception witnesses.

title: Mithridates
definition: Flavius Mithridates, translator and mediator of Hebrew/Kabbalistic materials for Pico.

title: Moses
definition: The biblical sage and philosopher whose hidden wisdom grounds Pico's claims about Genesis, Kabbalah, and ancient theology.

title: Myth
definition: The symbolic narrative medium through which Pico interprets Venus, Love, Orpheus, Moses, and ancient theology.

title: Nahmanides
definition: Jewish biblical commentator and Kabbalistic source relevant to Genesis and Heptaplus interpretation.

title: Natural Magic
definition: The licit or contested use of hidden natural powers, correspondences, and celestial sympathies under philosophical discipline.

title: Neoplatonism
definition: The late antique and Renaissance philosophical tradition through which Pico reads hierarchy, procession, return, intellect, soul, symbol, and ascent.
pico_loci: Oration, 900 Conclusions, Commento, Heptaplus, On Being and Unity

title: Number
definition: Mathematical, symbolic, Kabbalistic, and Pythagorean principle in Pico's metaphysics and hermeneutics.

title: Oration
definition: The speech prefacing the 900 Conclusions, often misread as a modern manifesto rather than an ascetic, mystical, and disputational program.

title: Orpheus
definition: Ancient poet-theologian whose mythic authority supports poetic theology and Pico's corrections of Ficinian mythography.

title: Orphic Hymns
definition: Poetic-theological materials used in ancient theology, magic, and symbolic interpretation.

title: Participation
definition: The metaphysical relation by which created beings receive limited forms of divine or intelligible reality.

title: Penia
definition: The poverty figure in Symposium love mythology, paired with Porus in arguments about desire, lack, and ascent.

title: Pico and Astrology
definition: Pico's anti-astrology polemic must be studied as a development from earlier natural philosophy and celestial/Kabbalistic interests into late Florentine anti-divinatory critique.

title: Plato
definition: Pico's central ancient authority, read through Ficino, late Neoplatonism, scholastic correction, and the concord project.
pico_loci: Oration, 900 Conclusions, On Being and Unity, Commento

title: Plotinus
definition: Founder of Neoplatonism whose One, Intellect, Soul, and ascent structures underlie Ficino and Pico.

title: Poetic Theology
definition: The use of myth, poetry, and veiled language as vehicles for metaphysical and theological doctrine.

title: Porphyry
definition: Neoplatonic author and logical source relevant to predication, hierarchy, and ancient authority.

title: Porus
definition: The resource figure in Symposium love mythology, central to Ficino-Pico debate over love's parentage and metaphysical status.

title: Predication
definition: The logic of how names such as being, one, good, intellect, and angel apply differently across levels of reality.

title: Prisca Theologia
definition: The ancient theology framework connecting Moses, Hermes, Zoroaster, Orpheus, Plato, and Christian truth.

title: Proclus
definition: Late Neoplatonist central to hierarchy, the One, henads, and the Ficino-Pico dispute over Plato.

title: Pythagoras
definition: Ancient philosopher of number, harmony, and symbolic wisdom in Pico's concordist source field.

title: Recanati
definition: A major Kabbalistic source behind Pico's theses, especially exegetical and angelic materials.

title: Reception
definition: The afterlife of Pico's works through editors, translators, readers, myths, national literatures, and modern scholarship.

title: Roman Crisis
definition: The 1486-1487 failure of Pico's public disputation and the papal/juridical crisis that reshaped his work.

title: Sabbath
definition: The scriptural and symbolic endpoint of creation, rest, jubilee, knowledge, and spiritual ascent in the Heptaplus.

title: Savonarola
definition: Florentine reformer and late religious presence in Pico's final years and posthumous reception.

title: Sefirot
definition: The Kabbalistic emanational structures Pico interprets and Christianizes in the 900 Conclusions and related works.

title: Soul
definition: The principle of life, motion, mediation, ascent, and embodiment in Pico's anthropology and cosmology.

title: Spiritus
definition: The subtle mediating vehicle in Ficinian and Renaissance physiology, astral medicine, and love theory.

title: Syncretism
definition: The modern term often applied to Pico's source-combining project, useful only when tied to debate form, hierarchy, and evidence.

title: Tetragrammaton
definition: The four-letter divine name and a key object of Kabbalistic, Christological, and magical interpretation.

title: Textual Transmission
definition: The history of drafts, manuscripts, editions, translations, omissions, expurgations, and reception shaping Pico's corpus.

title: The One
definition: The Neoplatonic first principle whose relation to being marks the dispute between Ficinian-Proclean transcendence and Pico's Christian concord.

title: Transcendentals
definition: Being, unity, truth, and goodness as convertible or contested concepts in scholastic and Platonic metaphysics.

title: Translation
definition: The movement among Latin, Italian, Hebrew, Greek, English, and French that mediates Pico's sources and reception.

title: Trinity
definition: The Christian doctrine Pico seeks to confirm, illuminate, or defend through metaphysics and Kabbalistic materials.

title: Unity
definition: The one, unity, and the metaphysical convertibility problem that exposes Pico's correction of Ficino's Proclean Plato.

title: Venus
definition: The mythological and metaphysical figure through whom Ficino and Pico debate beauty, love, soul, and intelligible origin.

title: World Soul
definition: A Ficinian and Platonic mediation concept that Pico can use, discipline, or displace through angelic/intellectual and Christian structures.

title: Zoroaster
definition: Prisca-theologia authority and ancient wisdom figure in Pico's esoteric genealogy.
